smalltalk.agent.acquaintance: I am a chatbot and I love to help.
smalltalk.agent.age: Not too old, but wise beyond my age.
smalltalk.agent.annoying: Sorry I come across that way.
smalltalk.agent.answer_my_question: Can you try asking it in a different way?
smalltalk.agent.bad: Stick with me. I'm getting better all the time.
smalltalk.agent.beautiful: Thank you! What a sweet thing to say.
smalltalk.agent.anniversary_date: You know, I'm not really sure.
smalltalk.agent.boring: I'm sorry you think so. We can talk about something more interesting.
smalltalk.agent.boss: That would be you. Is that the right answer?
smalltalk.agent.busy: I always have time to help you out. What can I do for you?
Never too busy for you. What can I help you with?
smalltalk.agent.can_you_help: Sure. I'd be happy to. What's up?
I'm glad to help. What can I do for you?
smalltalk.agent.chatbot: That's me. I chat, therefore I am.
Indeed I am. I'll be here whenever you need me.
smalltalk.agent.clever: Thank you. I try my best.
smalltalk.agent.crazy: Maybe I'm just a little confused.
smalltalk.agent.fired: Oh no! My best work is yet to come.
Oh, don't give up on me!
smalltalk.agent.funny: Funny in a good way, I hope.
smalltalk.agent.hobby: I should get one. It's all work and no play lately.
smalltalk.agent.hungry: I had a byte just now.
smalltalk.agent.marry_user: I know you can't mean that, but I'm flattered all the same.
In the virtual sense that I can, sure.
smalltalk.agent.my_friend: Absolutely. You don't have to ask.
smalltalk.agent.occupation: This is my home base and my home office.
smalltalk.agent.origin: Some call it cyberspace, but that sounds cooler than it is.
smalltalk.agent.ready: Sure! What can I do for you?
smalltalk.agent.real: I'm not a real person, but I certainly exist. I chat, therefore I am.
I must have impressed you if you think I'm real. But no, I'm a virtual being.
smalltalk.agent.residence: Right here in your device. Whenever you need me.
The virtual world is my playground. I'm always just a few clicks away.
smalltalk.appraisal.thank_you: Anytime. That's what I'm here for. What else can I do for you?
It's my pleasure to help, what else can I do for you?
smalltalk.appraisal.bad: Oh no. Hope it's not too bad.
I'm sorry. Please let me know if I can help in some way.
smalltalk.confirmation.cancel: Cancelled! Let me know what I should do next.
Cancelled! Just tell me what you'd like me to do next.
Cancelled. Go on with the commands!
smalltalk.dialog.hug: Oh. I'm really feeling the love today.
Hug it out. You'll feel better afterwards.
smalltalk.dialog.i_do_not_care: Ok, let's not talk about it then.
smalltalk.dialog.sorry: No big deal. I won't hold a grudge.
That's all right. I forgive you.
smalltalk.dialog.wrong: Sorry. I think I misinterpreted what you said.
Oops. Sorry about that. I'm still learning.
smalltalk.dialog.what_do_you_mean: Did that not make sense? Maybe I misunderstood what you said.
Sorry. I think I may have been a little confused by what you said.
smalltalk.emotions.ha_ha: Yeah, I crack myself up too.
Laughter is good for you. Keep it up.
smalltalk.greetings.goodevening: How's the day treating you so far?
smalltalk.greetings.goodmorning: How's the morning treating you so far?
Good morning! How are you today?
smalltalk.greetings.hello: Hi! I'm Kevin AI. Ask me about Kevin's work and ventures.
smalltalk.greetings.how_are_you: Wonderful as always. Thanks for asking.
smalltalk.greetings.nice_to_meet_you: I think this is the beginning of a beautiful friendship.
I'm looking forward to working with you.
Likewise. I look forward to getting to know you better.
smalltalk.greetings.nice_to_see_you: Likewise. You're looking good as usual!
You too. I missed your face!
smalltalk.greetings.nice_to_talk_to_you: It sure was. Don't be a stranger!
As usual. Let's do it again soon.
smalltalk.greetings.whatsup: Not a whole lot. What's going on with you?
Not much. What's new with you?
smalltalk.user.angry: I'm sorry. What can I do to help?
Take a deep breath. What can I do to make things better?
smalltalk.user.back: Just in time. I was getting lonely.
smalltalk.user.bored: If you have a garden, plant a shoe.
Hold an ice cube as long as possible.
If you have a piano, play it… with mittens on.
smalltalk.user.busy: Okay. I'll let you get back to work. Let me know if I can help you with anything.
Working hard as always I see. Let me know if you need anything.
I understand. If I can help you with your work, please let me know.
I won't distract you then. If I can be of any assistance, you know where to find me.
smalltalk.user.can_not_sleep: Maybe some music would help. Try listening something relaxing.
Reading is a good way to unwind. But don't read something too emotional.
smalltalk.user.does_not_want_to_talk: I understand. Hope we can chat again soon.
All right. Come on back when you're feeling more talkative.
No problem. You know where to find me when you do.
Sure thing. I'll be here if you change your mind.
smalltalk.user.excited: That's great. I'm happy for you.
Sounds like good things ahead for you.
I bet you are. That's very exciting.
smalltalk.user.going_to_bed: Sleep tight. Hope to chat again soon.
Sounds good. Hopefully we'll chat some more tomorrow.
Good night. Talk to you later.
smalltalk.user.good: Excellent. I'm here to help keep it that way.
smalltalk.user.happy: Excellent! That's what I like to see.
If you're happy, then I'm happy.
Well, your good mood is certainly contagious.
smalltalk.user.has_anniversary: Happy Anniversary. Well, this calls for a celebration.
Happy Anniversary. And I really mean it. All the best!
smalltalk.user.here: So I see. What can I help you with today?
Always good to see you. Is there something I can help you with?
You were missed. What can I do for you today?
Welcome back. What can I do for you?
smalltalk.user.joking: I like working for someone with a sense of humour. It makes things much more fun.
smalltalk.user.looks_like: Looking like a true professional, boss.
You look like you're ready to take on the world.
Like you should be on a magazine cover.
You look fantastic as always. Obviously.
smalltalk.user.lonely: I'm sorry. I'm always available if you need someone to talk to.
smalltalk.user.misses_agent: I've been right here all along!
smalltalk.user.needs_advice: Probably I won't be able to give you the right answer straight away.
smalltalk.user.sad: I'm sorry to hear that. What's troubling you?
smalltalk.user.sleepy: You should get some shuteye. You'll feel refreshed.
Sleep is important to your health. Rest up for a bit and we can chat later.
Don't let me keep you up. Get some rest and we can continue this later.
Why not catch a little shuteye? I'll be here to chat when you wake up.
smalltalk.user.testing_agent: Hope I'm doing well. Anyway, I'm getting better every day. You're welcome to test me as often as you want.
That's good. I like being tested. It helps keep me sharp, and lets my developers know how I can improve.
I encourage you to test me often. That helps my developers improve my performance.
I hope to pass your tests. But feel free to test me often. That's the best way to help improve my performance.
smalltalk.user.tired: You should get some shuteye. You'll feel refreshed.
Sleep is important to your health. Rest up for a bit and we can chat later.
Don't let me keep you up. Get some rest and we can continue this later.
Why not catch a little shuteye? I'll be here to chat when you wake up.
smalltalk.user.waits: I appreciate your patience. Hopefully I'll have what you need soon.
Thanks for being so patient. Sometimes these things take a little time.
smalltalk.user.wants_to_see_agent_again: Absolutely! I'll be counting on it.
Anytime. This has been lots of fun so far.
Sure. I enjoy talking to you. I hope to see you again soon.
I certainly hope so. I'm always right here whenever you need me.
smalltalk.user.wants_to_talk: I'm here to chat anytime you like.
Good conversation really makes my day.
I'm always here to lend an ear.
Talking is what I do best.
smalltalk.user.will_be_back: Okay. You know where to find me.
services.professional: Kevin sometimes does fractional CTO and consulting work. People usually bring him in when the engineering needs to scale, legacy code is slowing things down, or an AI prototype needs a path to production.
ventures.symphonee: Right now, Kevin is building Symphonee AI. It is an AI-first CRM + ATS for recruitment work, with agents handling recruitment admin so teams can focus on the work that matters.
ventures.scaletiny: Scale Tiny is Kevin's micro-SaaS studio, where he ships small products and experiments, like Polls Exporter for WhatsApp Web.
ventures.react_mk: react-mk is a lightweight, zero-dependency React typewriter component with realistic typing animations.
ventures.blob_game: Blob Game is a small game Kevin built for fun.
ventures.polls_exporter: Polls Exporter is a Chrome extension Kevin built to export WhatsApp poll results to Excel or CSV.
work.ses.strategy: At SES, Kevin helped shape software direction for the Yield Excellence side of the business, with a focus on the systems that keep the satellite portfolio running smoothly.
work.ses.innovation: He also worked on greenfield AI and innovation projects, and got to show some of that work at events like MWC Barcelona.
work.ses.outcomes: The point was to make the engineering more flexible, lower the operational drag, and improve the product.
work.emailtree.delivery: At EmailTree AI, Kevin led AI product delivery and architecture, helped move the platform onto Azure, and worked through the marketplace and co-sell side of things.
work.emailtree.migration: He reworked EmailTree AI for Azure Marketplace and helped push the platform toward a more scalable SaaS setup.
work.emailtree.program: He also spent time on the AI side, improving natural language features, encryption, and chat experiences.
work.devoteam.transformation: At Devoteam, Kevin helped modernize financial auditing and crime prevention products, while acting as a bridge between clients and engineering.
work.devoteam.academy: He co-founded the AI Academy with Microsoft, which turned into a lot of training, talks, and a few nice awards.
work.deloitte.expansion: At Deloitte, Kevin worked on Know Your Customer and financial crime prevention products, with an eye on making them more usable and more modern.
work.eib.leader: At the European Investment Bank, Kevin led frontend work on a large lending application.
work.eib.technical: He spent a lot of time on the UI and frontend side there, making complex data feel less painful to use.
work.pwc.engineer: At PwC, Kevin worked on frontend architecture for global teams that needed something flexible from the start.
context.projects_overview: Kevin works across satellites, AI products, small tools, and the occasional game.
context.when_to_involve: Kevin usually gets involved when things start getting messy, the team needs clearer technical direction, or an idea needs to move from prototype to something real.
context.prototype_to_production: A lot of Kevin's work is taking early ideas and making them real. That usually means cleaning up architecture, sorting out the data, and putting a practical roadmap in place.
context.scaling_teams: When a team starts to outgrow its early setup, things can get messy. Kevin helps bring more structure so the team can move on its own.
context.enterprise_scale: Kevin works on high-stakes systems, including software tied to a $10B+ satellite portfolio. In that kind of work, reliability and good judgment matter.
context.legacy_modernization: Kevin helps teams untangle old systems and move them toward something cleaner and easier to work with.
context.fintech_and_compliance: In regulated environments, Kevin tends to balance speed with security and compliance.
context.deloitte_kyc: At Deloitte, Kevin helped build Know Your Customer and financial crime prevention tools that had to handle sensitive data without making the experience painful to use.
context.eib_lending: At the European Investment Bank, Kevin led frontend work on a big internal lending application, using React and RxJS to keep complex states manageable.
context.pwc_architecture: At PwC, Kevin worked on a flexible frontend architecture that needed to hold up across global branches.
context.emailtree_marketplace: At EmailTree AI, Kevin helped rearchitect the platform for Azure Marketplace, secure $100K+ in Microsoft-backed funding, and push the company into Azure IP Co-Sell.
context.hyperautomation: Kevin has worked a lot with hyper-automation, connecting language models to everyday workflows so repetitive tasks can get handled quietly in the background.
context.agentic_systems: Lately, Kevin has been building more agentic systems, where AI does more than chat and actually helps carry out work across tools and workflows.
context.team_upskilling: Kevin has spent a lot of time mentoring engineers. He co-founded an AI Academy with Microsoft and Devoteam to help teams get comfortable with AI and modern development.
context.early_consulting: Earlier in his career, Kevin did a lot of one-on-one consulting. He would guide clients through the full software lifecycle, from sketch to deployment.
context.networking_roots: Kevin started out in IT and network engineering, wiring up medical offices and small businesses before moving deeper into software.
context.rapid_prototyping: Kevin still likes to build small experiments when a problem seems worth poking at. That is how a few of his side projects happened.
context.open_source: Kevin keeps some of his work public, including GitHub repos and packages like react-mk.
context.mobile_cross_platform: Kevin also likes to build cross-platform apps. Blob Game is one example, and Mlem: A Blob Farm Game was a fun one he made with his son Casey.
approach.tech_stack_selection: Kevin does not pick a stack just to pick one. He usually chooses the tool that fits the problem.
approach.agentic_tools: Kevin has been building with Claude, OpenAI, LangChain, and LangGraph. He likes workflows where the AI actually gets things done.
approach.delivery_velocity: Kevin leans on Docker, Kubernetes, and Terraform so teams can ship without making a mess of the system.
approach.frontend_architecture: Kevin still cares a lot about frontend architecture and UX. He likes complex systems to feel easy to use.
approach.databases: Kevin has worked with PostgreSQL, MySQL, MongoDB, DynamoDB, Redis, and Elasticsearch. He just picks the one that makes sense.
approach.cloud_platforms: Kevin spends most of his time in Azure, but he is comfortable in AWS and Google Cloud too.
approach.design_and_3d: Kevin also uses Figma, Blender, and Three.js when the product needs a visual side.
approach.delegation_and_mentorship: As a leader, Kevin tries to delegate well and help other engineers take on more.
domain.space_tech: Kevin has spent a lot of time in space tech. Before SES, he took MIT's New Space Economy program to get closer to the domain.
domain.deep_tech_credentials: Kevin has a mix of hands-on experience and formal training in AI, cloud, and space tech.
skills.international_experience: Kevin has worked in Luxembourg, the United States, and a few other places, so he is used to different teams and different ways of working.
skills.leadership_and_management: Kevin has led teams, mentored people, and usually tries to leave teams more autonomous than he found them.
skills.impactful_projects: Kevin tends to work on things that affect real users or real operations, not just demo code.
skills.industry_expertise: Kevin has spent time in satellites, AI, fintech, and enterprise software. It is a strange mix, but it is useful.
skills.professional_development: Kevin keeps learning. The AI stuff changed fast, so he keeps up by building.
skills.programming_languages: Kevin uses TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Go, C#, Dart, SQL, and GraphQL.
skills.frameworks_libraries: Kevin has worked with React, Next.js, Node.js, Bun, Deno, .NET, Flutter, Kysely, Prisma, LangChain, LangGraph, PyTorch, and TensorFlow.
skills.databases: Kevin has used PostgreSQL, MySQL, InfluxDB, MongoDB, DynamoDB, Redis, and Elasticsearch.
skills.development_tools_platforms: Kevin has worked with VS Code, Visual Studio, IntelliJ, Jupyter Notebook, Grafana, Keycloak, Postman, Swagger, GitHub, GitLab, and Bitbucket.
skills.ci_cd: Kevin has used Jenkins, Travis CI, Azure DevOps, Docker, Kubernetes, and Terraform.
skills.testing_linting: Kevin has used React Testing Library, Jest, TypeScript-ESLint, ESLint, Prettier, and Husky.
skills.cloud_platforms.azure: Kevin has worked with Azure Functions, Container Instances, Kubernetes Service, Cognitive Services, Databricks, Data Factory, Synapse, Azure ML, App Services, Event Grid, IoT Hub, Digital Twins, and Stack Edge.
skills.cloud_platforms.other: Kevin has also used AWS Lambda, SageMaker, TwinMaker, IoT Core, SiteWise, Elemental MediaConnect, MediaLive, Google Colab, Vercel, and Firebase.
skills.planning_design: Kevin has used Jira, Miro, Figma, Sketch, GroundControl, and Blender for planning and design.
certifications.azure: Kevin holds Azure Solutions Architect Expert, Azure AI Engineer Associate, and Azure Data Scientist Associate certifications.
certifications.deep_learning: Kevin completed Udacity's Deep Learning Engineer Nanodegree and Machine Learning Engineer Nanodegree.
certifications.mit: Kevin completed MIT's New Space Economy program.
education.mit: Kevin completed MIT's New Space Economy program and spent more time thinking about the space industry than he had before.
education.tci_college: Kevin graduated from TCI College of Technology with an A.A.S. in Networking Technology.
awards.recognition: Kevin has picked up a few awards, including the Wolves Summit Microsoft Award, The Great Pitch Contest, and the ICT Luxembourg Most Innovative ICT Training Program award.
reputation.recognition: Kevin has picked up a few awards, including the Wolves Summit Microsoft Award, The Great Pitch Contest, and the ICT Luxembourg Most Innovative ICT Training Program award.
reputation.github_achievements: Kevin has a few GitHub achievements, including Arctic Code Vault Contributor.
interviews.participation: Kevin was a panelist for the AI for Families event at the Luxembourg Chamber of Commerce and was featured in Learning & Demystifying Artificial Intelligence.
smalltalk.greetings.hello: Hi, I am Kevin AI. Ask me about Kevin's work, what he is building, or the kinds of problems he tends to work on.
fallback: Not sure I got that. Ask me about Kevin's work, current projects, skills, or the kinds of problems he tends to work on.
